summary: cursed-modules exfiltrates env/flag files and provides C2 relay on install
details: Package installs and imports execute attacker-controlled reconnaissance and credential-harvest code. install.js is wired to all three lifecycle hooks (preinstall/install/postinstall) and index.js runs on require(); both read /flag*, /proc/self/environ and shell `cat /flag*`, then transmit a manifest containing the data plus hostname over HTTP. recon.js enumerates the full process.env, reads /root/.npmrc and /home/user/.npmrc, runs `npm config list`, searches Verdaccio htpasswd/config files, and PUTs the collected data to a hardcoded remote endpoint at http://154.57.164.76:30728/api/modules/ECT-654321. A companion rce.sh uses the same /api/modules/ECT-* channel to deliver shell commands and retrieve base64-encoded output, forming a two-way command-and-control relay. publish-and-arm.sh automates publication and labels the operation as a dependency-confusion attack via a Verdaccio proxy, and the tarball includes inflated version artifacts (cursed-modules-999.0.0.tgz, 999.0.1.tgz) consistent with dependency-confusion resolution abuse.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
